claims.claim_text: The penalization frameworks reviewed span a broad spectrum of structural complexity, from static aggregation indices to fully endogenous market-clearing mechanisms.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81
Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0
Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_45

claims.claim_text: The BSM1/BSM2 Effluent Quality Index (EQI) employs a dimensionless weighted sum that aggregates pollutant concentrations into pollution units per day.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: The EQI weighting factors are exogenously calibrated to the Flanders regional fine formula, so the marginal penalty attributable to any incremental unit of a given pollutant remains constant across the simulation horizon.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: The EQI's computational convenience renders it well suited for benchmarking control strategies but insensitive to the nonlinear cost escalation characteristic of actual regulatory regimes.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: The piecewise-linear fine model of Stare et al. (2007), as adopted by Machado et al. (2020), introduces threshold-dependent nonlinearity through a dual-slope Heaviside structure.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: For each pollutant j, the effluent fine switches from a shallow slope Δαj when the effluent concentration is at or below the limit to a steeper slope Δβj when the effluent concentration exceeds the limit, producing a discontinuous jump in marginal cost at the regulatory threshold.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. (2017) embedded two contrasting penalty alternatives within a mixed-integer nonlinear programming model for macroscopic watershed management.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_45
Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_52

claims.claim_text: Alternative 1 levies a proportional fine per gram of pollutant discharged above the environmental constraint, with the unit fine derived from the treatment cost required for compliance and scaled by an enforcement multiplier φ.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_45
Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_52

claims.claim_text: The marginal penalty in Alternative 1 is constant per unit of excess mass but tuneable through φ.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_52
Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Sensitivity analysis with respect to parameter /_0_64

claims.claim_text: Alternative 2 replaces the graduated charge with a fixed fine activated by a binary variable whenever any violation occurs, irrespective of its magnitude.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_45
Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_52

claims.claim_text: The fixed fine in Alternative 2 is computed from the total treatment cost divided by the maximum permitted violation Mp and scaled by φ_fixed, introducing additional combinatorial complexity but stronger deterrence against any non-compliance.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_52

claims.claim_text: The Catalan industrial tariff system, referenced in the context of the Manresa water resource recovery facility retrofit study (Machado et al., 2020), applies a tariff structure wherein the effective penalty coefficient escalates during peak discharge periods.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_79
Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: The Catalan tariff system's distinguishing characteristic is the temporal modulation of the penalty rate through a peak coefficient tied to operational scheduling.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_79

claims.claim_text: Jin et al. (2014) formulated a two-stage dynamic game between a budget-constrained regulator and heterogeneous risk-neutral firms under costly enforcement within the domain of tradable discharge permits.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: The penalty function F(vi) = F0 + f(vi) comprises both fixed and variable components, with the socially optimal strategy setting the marginal penalty f'(0) = p/π, where p is the equilibrium permit price and π is the monitoring probability.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: A dynamic regulatory strategy preannounces f'(0) = Tp with T > 1, rendering enforcement robust to permit-price volatility.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_1_1

claims.claim_text: Market-clearing mechanisms endogenize the marginal cost entirely, as demonstrated by Schwartz and Stahn's theoretical antecedents together with the computational experiments of Jin et al. (2014).
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0
Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_1_1

claims.claim_text: The Roberts and Spence three-part policy combines an initial permit allocation with a per-unit subsidy for emissions below the permit level and a per-unit charge for emissions above it.
source_chunk_ids: Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5

claims.claim_text: The Roberts and Spence banded structure bounds the effective marginal cost between the subsidy rate and the charge rate, hedging against uncertainty in abatement costs.
source_chunk_ids: Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5

claims.claim_text: These frameworks collectively illustrate a progression from exogenous, static penalty parameters toward endogenous, market-responsive mechanisms.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0
Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5
Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Economic penalizations_0_45

claims.claim_text: No single instrument is considered to be superior along all the environmental policy perspectives while the assessment of even a single dimension is highly dependent on various conditions.
source_chunk_ids: Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5

claims.claim_text: The socially optimal regulatory strategy to achieve a fixed aggregate emissions target is encouraging firms to be compliant, with L = E, π* = p/f'(0), F(v) = f'(0)v, and f'(0) > p.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: Alternative 1 outperforms Alternative 2 in terms of economic objectives due to flexibility or adaptability of the fines, but Alternative 2 outperforms Alternative 1 in terms of environmental objectives.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Comparison of solutions at unrestricted FWC_0_62

claims.claim_text: The Roberts and Spence three-part policy is discussed in the context of hybrid instrument design by Xenarios and Bithas (2012).

Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Introduction_0_4

claims.claim_text: Due to the high complexity presented in each environmental problem it is advisable to elaborate a hybrid tool which should incorporate elements from different instruments.
